name: gofmt_check
description: Check Go source file(s) syntax using 'gofmt -e'. Reports whether files have valid Go syntax. Supports single files, directories (checks all .go files recursively), uses find to locate .go files excluding vendor/ and .git/.
when_to_use: Use after editing Go source files to quickly verify syntax before building or running tests. Replaces the pattern of 'cd <project> && gofmt -e <file> > /dev/null && echo "Syntax OK" || echo "Syntax errors"' with a single script call. For single files or entire directories.

parameters.name: project_root
parameters.description: Root directory of the Go project (e.g. /app)

parameters.name: target
parameters.description: File or directory to check (default: '.' for the entire project). For directories, recursively checks all .go files.

examples.expected: Check all Go files under /app for syntax errors.
Check all Go files under /app/pkg/ for syntax errors.

cost_saving_rationale: Replaces 2-3 separate commands (cd to project, gofmt -e, status check) with a single script call. Directory mode eliminates repetitive gofmt calls for each file. Provides a clean summary instead of raw error output, reducing token usage.
